---
title: "Report Title: Descriptive Subtitle"
subtitle: "Synaptic Four Technical Report SF-TR-YYYY-NNN"
author:
  - name: Author One
    affiliation: Synaptic Four
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
    email: author@synapticfour.com
  - name: Author Two
    affiliation: Partner Organization
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
date: last-modified
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"
version: "1.0.0"
report-id: SF-TR-YYYY-NNN
report-category: architecture
keywords:
  - bioinformatics
  - FAIR data
  - GA4GH
  - genomics
abstract: |
  Provide a concise abstract (150–300 words) summarising the problem addressed,
  approach taken, principal findings or contributions, and intended audience.
  The abstract should stand alone for discovery in catalogues and search engines.
license: CC BY 4.0
doi: ""
zenodo: ""
github-release: ""
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
bibliography: references.bib
csl: ../../csl/ieee.csl
---

# Executive Summary {.unnumbered}

Brief overview for readers who need the essential conclusions without full technical detail. One to three paragraphs.

# Introduction

Introduce the context, motivation, and scope of this report. State what the reader will learn and who should read it.

# Background

Provide domain context, terminology, and assumptions. Define acronyms on first use.

# Problem Statement

Articulate the specific technical, architectural, or methodological problem this report addresses. Be precise about constraints and success criteria.

# Related Work

Survey relevant standards, prior implementations, academic literature, and competing or complementary approaches. Position Synaptic Four's contribution relative to existing work.

# System Architecture

Describe the high-level architecture. Include diagrams where helpful.

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: Example architecture diagram (replace with project-specific content)
flowchart LR
  A[Data Sources] --> B[Ingestion Layer]
  B --> C[Processing]
  C --> D[API / Access Layer]
  D --> E[Consumers]
```

Document major components, interfaces, data flows, and design decisions.

# Implementation

Detail implementation choices: languages, frameworks, libraries, configuration patterns, and key algorithms. Reference source repositories where applicable.

# Deployment

Describe deployment topology, infrastructure requirements, operational procedures, and environment-specific considerations.

# Security Considerations

Address threat model, authentication and authorisation, encryption, audit logging, data residency, and compliance-relevant controls.

# Performance Considerations

Report benchmarks, scalability characteristics, resource requirements, and known bottlenecks where measured.

# Limitations

State known limitations, unsupported use cases, and assumptions that may not hold in all environments.

# Future Work

Outline planned improvements, open questions, and areas for community or collaborator contribution.

# Conclusion

Summarise principal contributions and recommendations. State actionable next steps for readers.

# Acknowledgements {.unnumbered}

Acknowledge funding sources, collaborators, reviewers, and upstream open-source projects.

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::

# Appendices {.unnumbered}

## Appendix A: Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| DRS  | Data Repository Service (GA4GH) |
| FAIR | Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable |

## Appendix B: Configuration Reference

Include supplementary tables, API excerpts, or configuration examples that support the main text but would interrupt narrative flow.

## Appendix C: Change Log

| Version | Date | Summary |
|---------|------|---------|
| 1.0.0   | YYYY-MM-DD | Initial release |
